verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: A quality/{target} causes creation of a financial change event or cost record
verbGlossary.ORIGINATED FROM: {source} traces its origin back to a field quality or coordination {target}
verbGlossary.COSTS: {source} carries the dollar cost attributable to {target}
verbGlossary.BACKCHARGED FOR: {source} charges a subcontractor for rework caused by quality/safety deficiencies
verbGlossary.REMEDIATION COST OF: {source} entry represents the actual dollars spent to remediate a field {target} or incident
verbGlossary.MEASURED BY: {source} rework exposure is quantified by a quality/safety metric
verbGlossary.REALLOCATED FOR: {source} transfers funds to cover costs arising from a quality or safety issue
verbGlossary.DRAWN DOWN BY: {source} reserve is consumed to pay for costs arising from a quality/{target}
verbGlossary.ACCELERATED BY: {source}rate increases faster due to quality/{target} rates
verbGlossary.FUNDED BY: {source}is paid for through a specific financial instrument
verbGlossary.CLASSIFIES COST OF: {source} or WBS code categorizes the financial impact of a quality issue by trade or work type
verbGlossary.WITHHELD UNTIL: {source} are held back pending resolution of outstanding punch items
verbGlossary.RELEASED UPON: {source}are released when {target}s pass or punch items are resolved
verbGlossary.GATED BY: {source} is blocked until a quality metric threshold is met
verbGlossary.DISPUTED BY: {source}is contested due to an open {target} or non-conformance notice
verbGlossary.HELD FOR: An owner invoice withholds amounts pending punch list completion
verbGlossary.VALIDATES WORK IN: An inspection confirms that work billed on a schedule of values line is acceptably complete
verbGlossary.GATES PAYMENT ON: An inspection item or third-party inspection must pass before payment is released on an invoice
verbGlossary.GATES BILLING ON: A scheduled inspection must pass before an owner pay application can be submitted
verbGlossary.INCURS: {target} or violation directly generates financial costs
verbGlossary.FORCES: {source}requires a budget modification to cover unplanned costs
verbGlossary.CLAIMED AGAINST: {target} or test failure results in a financial claim against a responsible party
verbGlossary.CLAIMED FOR: {source}of costs caused by {target} or design coordination issues
verbGlossary.REJECTED VIA: {source}test report triggers a commitment change order to replace non-compliant materials
verbGlossary.CHARGED TO: {source}plan task's cost is recorded against a specific direct cost line item
verbGlossary.ERODED BY: {source}project budget margin is reduced by cumulative rework costs
verbGlossary.PREDICTED BY: {source}is forecast based on a leading quality indicator like {target} density
verbGlossary.DEGRADED BY: {source} worsens due to rework and {target} costs
verbGlossary.CORRELATED WITH: {source}rate metric shows statistical association with a quality/safety frequency metric
verbGlossary.INFLATED BY: An estimate at completion increases due to accumulated rework cost
verbGlossary.DISRUPTED BY: {source}projection is disrupted by unplanned {target} or rework expenditures
verbGlossary.INCREASED BY: {source}cost grows due to {target}s requiring additional change orders
verbGlossary.ADJUSTED FOR: {source} is revised to account for observed quality/safety trends
verbGlossary.DELAYED BY: {source} processing time increases due to open {target} notices blocking approval
verbGlossary.DELAYS: Slow observation resolution delays the financial closeout of associated cost items
verbGlossary.GATES RELEASE OF: {source}must be satisfied before held funds are released
verbGlossary.ESCALATES: {source}rate metric drives up overall project financial exposure
verbGlossary.RESPONSIBLE FOR: {source}identifies the vendor financially accountable for field findings
verbGlossary.VERIFIED BY: {source}are validated through material test reports before acceptance
verbGlossary.PRICED FROM: {source}cost estimate derives from {target} or non-conformance finding
verbGlossary.CHARGED BACK TO: {source}cost is charged back to the responsible subcontractor's commitment
